No specific dorm quality complaints found in data

Catholic identity shapes campus community and social environment
Students describe a tight-knit community feel vs. large state schools

key_points: Large merit scholarships offered but total cost still very high
Even with $37k scholarship, students call UD 'stupid expensive'
Merit aid covers 30%+ of students but net price remains a concern

Holy shit don't go to Dayton. $112k is crippling. Go to Ohio University, $52k is already scary.
University of Dayton - important [for demonstrated interest, with merit aid for 30%+ of students]

key_points: Smaller class sizes repeatedly cited as genuine advantage over state schools
Not considered prestigious outside Ohio; limited national name recognition
Engineering and business programs mentioned most; CS seen as mediocre
key_quotes: I'm from Ohio and wouldn't say the University of Dayton is a very respected university worth paying a lot of extra money to attend. It's not prestigious enough to be well-known outside of the state.
None of these are known for their CS programs
Dayton has smaller classes and a better community aspect

Campus described positively by those who visited; community feel noted

Extra $40-60k over state schools not justified by outcomes, per students
